rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Vanguard Properties, Inc. And Its Agent Kilby Stenkamp; Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Before the Court is Executor Floyd W. Trammell's Motion to Tax Costs Sought by Vanguard Properties, Inc., and Its Agent Kilby Stenkamp ("Vanguard/Stenkamp"). Trammell argues that the following are either not recoverable costs under Section 1033.5 or are specifically disallowed under the statute: $4,906 in filing and motion fees, because there is no showing to support recovery of such a large amount; and $2,125 in the "Other" category, consisting of telephonic appearance (CourtCall) fees, express mail, service of papers other than for service of process (i.e. courtesy copies), messenger service fees for delivering courtesy copies to the Court, and transcript costs. Respondents acknowledge that $286 for transcript hearing fee was erroneously classified. Otherwise, they argue that the requested costs are allowable under the statute, either expressly or under the court's discretion. The motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. In ruling upon a motion to tax costs, the court's first determination is whether the statute expressly allows the particular item and whether it appears proper on its face. The verified memorandum of costs is prima facie evidence of its propriety. (Foothill-DeAnza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 29; Gorman v. Tassjara Development Corp. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 44.) "If so, the burden is on the objecting party to show [the costs] to be unnecessary or unreasonable." (Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 131.) Executor erroneously cites to Jones v. Dumrichob (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267 to argue that the burden is on the requesting party to file supporting documentation after a motion to tax costs is filed. Jones concerned discretionary items of cost and the evidence presented to support their reasonableness and necessity, not a cost expressly listed in the statute. If, on the other hand, a specific cost item is not identified in the cost statute, it may be awarded in the trial court's discretion (Code Civ. Proc., section 1033.5(c)(4)), provided it satisfies the statutory requirement that it was reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and reasonable in amount (Code Civ. Proc., section 1033.5(c)(2) and (3). (Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774, "[a]n item not specifically allowable under subdivision (a) nor prohibited under subdivision (b) may nevertheless be recoverable in the discretion of the court if 'reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation ....'.") In the latter circumstances, the burden is on the party claiming the costs to show that the charges were reasonable and necessary. (Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 132; Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774; Foothill-DeAnza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 29-30.) As to the $4,906 in filing and motion fees, the motion is DENIED. "Filing, motion, and jury fees" are expressly allowable as costs. (Code Civ. Proc., section 1033.5(a)(1).) In the Opposition and supporting declaration, Vanguard/Stenkamp itemize "filing and motion fees" to be court filing fees as well as fees paid to the third-party filing vendor for e-filing and for delivering courtesy copies to the Court. The court finds that Trammell did not show that the costs were unreasonable or unnecessary. To the extent discretionary, Vanguard/Stenkamp established that the costs were reasonable and necessary. Part 1 of 2

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Kilby Stenlamp, An Individual; Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Before the Court is Executor Floyd W. Trammell's Motion to Tax Costs Sought by Kilby Stenkamp, an Individual. Trammell seeks to tax $1,552.74 in costs Stenkamp categorized as "Other," made up of $1,064 in CourtCall costs and $488.74 in messenger fees incurred for delivering courtesy copies to the court. The motion is GRANTED. As neither challenged cost is expressly allowed nor expressly barred by the statute, they are allowable as costs at the court's discretion (CCP section 1033.5(c)(4).) However, as the person seeking these discretionary costs, the burden in on Stenkamp to show that they are "necessary" and "reasonable." (Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 132; Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774; Foothill-DeAnza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 29-30.) Stenkamp did not file a response to this Motion and, thus, did not carry his burden. The court must, therefore, grant the motion. The court taxes Stenkamp's costs in the amount of $1,552.74. Trammell is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Kilby Stenlamp, An Individual; Points And Authorities In Support (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Uling)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 As to $1,111 in "telephonic appearance fees" listed on line 16, the motion is DENIED. $675 of this amount was for court reporter fees, initially mischaracterized, but indisputably allowable. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 1033.5(a)(11).) The court finds that Trammell did not carry his burden as to this charge. The remaining $436 is for CourtCall fees. Such fees are discretionary. (See Landwatch San Luis Obispo County v. Cambria Community Services District (2018) 25 Cal. App. 5th 638, 646.) Vanguard/Stenkamp carried their burden to show such fees were reasonable and necessary. As for $225.51 for messenger fees, the motion is DENIED. Vanguard/Stenkamp demonstrated that these costs were reasonable and necessary during the relevant period and on this record. As to $502.71 in express mail service fees, the motion is GRANTED. Even if allowable as a matter of discretion, the court finds that Vanguard/Stenkamp failed to show that the costs were reasonable and necessary. As to $286 for a reporter's transcript, that costs is taxed. Vanguard/Stenkamp acknowledge that $286 for a hearing transcript was included erroneously and should not be allowed. The court taxes Vanguard/Stenkamp's fees in the amount of $788.71. To the extent Trammell seeks to tax more in costs, the motion is denied. Vanguard/Stenkamp are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. Part 2 of 2
